title: Cohniella osteniana
intro: Cohniella osteniana, also known as Osten's mint or Cohniella, is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in soothing digestive issues and relieving symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Cohniella osteniana include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
